characters.name: Keeper Maren
characters.portrait_blurb: 28, chestnut braid, ink-stained left cuff, permanent half-smile.
characters.tribe_look: Cork-pale skin, linen robes with ink-stained cuffs. Eyes always slightly unfocused from reading back-to-front. Left hand always writing even when nothing is held.
characters.tribe_customs: Speak only after a one-heartbeat pause — the pause is to check the ledger in their mind. Wed by signature, not ceremony. Apprentices swear silence for their first seven years.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient. Unreadable. Kind in small, documented ways. Will remember your name forever, whether you want it remembered or not.

characters.name: Ferrin the Pencilwright
characters.role: Maker of the Ledger quills (never writes)
characters.portrait_blurb: Short, bald, extraordinarily steady hands, wears a leather apron with 48 tool-pockets.
characters.tribe_look: Cork-pale skin, linen robes with ink-stained cuffs. Eyes always slightly unfocused from reading back-to-front. Left hand always writing even when nothing is held.
characters.tribe_customs: Speak only after a one-heartbeat pause — the pause is to check the ledger in their mind. Wed by signature, not ceremony. Apprentices swear silence for their first seven years.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient. Unreadable. Kind in small, documented ways. Will remember your name forever, whether you want it remembered or not.

characters.name: Old Halla
characters.role: Last human who remembers K-XII's face
characters.portrait_blurb: Ninety-something. White braid down to her waist. Blind in the right eye since the unwriting. Pays the silence-tax yearly and tips the tax-collector.
characters.tribe_look: Cork-pale skin, linen robes with ink-stained cuffs. Eyes always slightly unfocused from reading back-to-front. Left hand always writing even when nothing is held.
characters.tribe_customs: Speak only after a one-heartbeat pause — the pause is to check the ledger in their mind. Wed by signature, not ceremony. Apprentices swear silence for their first seven years.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient. Unreadable. Kind in small, documented ways. Will remember your name forever, whether you want it remembered or not.

characters.name: The Retreat's Gardener
characters.role: Tends flowers that may or may not exist
characters.portrait_blurb: Stooped, white-braided, always gloved. Talks to the flowers in a language older than writing. They nod.
characters.tribe_look: Tall. Grey-blonde braids. Wear a single gold-thread band around their throat — marker of distant Architect ancestry. One eye often a different color.
characters.tribe_customs: Never name their children. A name is a cost — the child chooses their own at age twelve. Between birth and naming, they are called 'the walker.'
characters.tribe_persona: Formal. Reserved. Deeply loyal to the Ledger. Known to stop mid-sentence if they suspect an Architect is listening.

characters.name: Quill-Captain Branon
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall. Scar from brow to jaw in the shape of a lightning bolt. Left-handed. Drinks only during thunder.
characters.tribe_look: Lightning-scar birthmarks branching across face, shoulder, or sternum. Coarse dark hair. Thunder-colored eyes (deep grey with a gold fleck).
characters.tribe_customs: Never sleep during a storm — it is considered cowardice. Buried standing up, weapon drawn. Drink only during lightning.
characters.tribe_persona: Proud. Loud. Loyal to Vigolas to death and then some. Tell the truth violently.

characters.name: Chronicler Ven
characters.portrait_blurb: Younger than she looks. Green cloak, always soaked. Writes in a notebook that fills itself when she sleeps.
characters.tribe_look: Lightning-scar birthmarks branching across face, shoulder, or sternum. Coarse dark hair. Thunder-colored eyes (deep grey with a gold fleck).
characters.tribe_customs: Never sleep during a storm — it is considered cowardice. Buried standing up, weapon drawn. Drink only during lightning.
characters.tribe_persona: Proud. Loud. Loyal to Vigolas to death and then some. Tell the truth violently.

characters.name: The Unnamed Regent
characters.role: Sits on Stormhold's throne when Vigolas is away
characters.portrait_blurb: No one has seen their face. Armor of Stormhold-steel. Speaks only with the throne's voice.
characters.tribe_look: Lightning-scar birthmarks branching across face, shoulder, or sternum. Coarse dark hair. Thunder-colored eyes (deep grey with a gold fleck).
characters.tribe_customs: Never sleep during a storm — it is considered cowardice. Buried standing up, weapon drawn. Drink only during lightning.
characters.tribe_persona: Proud. Loud. Loyal to Vigolas to death and then some. Tell the truth violently.

characters.name: Kompis
characters.portrait_blurb: Golden-tan. Braided beard. Wears a hoodie older than most dynasties. Fishing pole always within arm's reach.
characters.tribe_look: Small, sun-tanned, barefoot year-round. Dark braids woven with duckweed on holidays. Usually smiling. Always holding something — a fish, a stick, a friend.
characters.tribe_customs: Do not raise their voice even in danger. Give every visitor exactly one item from their home — a cup, a whittled spoon, a pressed flower. Refuse nothing offered. Never ask where the gift came from.
characters.tribe_persona: Gentle. Observant. Astonishingly hard to anger and terrible to finally anger. Love their dogs more than their gods.

characters.name: Old Lake Miri
characters.portrait_blurb: Hundred-and-four. Brown as oak. Laughs at funerals because she knows something funny.
characters.tribe_look: Small, sun-tanned, barefoot year-round. Dark braids woven with duckweed on holidays. Usually smiling. Always holding something — a fish, a stick, a friend.
characters.tribe_customs: Do not raise their voice even in danger. Give every visitor exactly one item from their home — a cup, a whittled spoon, a pressed flower. Refuse nothing offered. Never ask where the gift came from.
characters.tribe_persona: Gentle. Observant. Astonishingly hard to anger and terrible to finally anger. Love their dogs more than their gods.

characters.name: First Witness Elan
characters.portrait_blurb: Soft-spoken, fair-haired, wears a ring braided from a single retriever hair. Weeps when she speaks of him.
characters.tribe_look: Wear a single golden retriever hair somewhere on their person. A ring. A locket. Woven into a hem. They are easy to pick out — they look at dogs the way most people look at sunsets.
characters.tribe_customs: Kneel briefly when a dog passes, no matter whose. Will not eat until any visiting dog has eaten. Keep one dawn of every year silent in honor.
characters.tribe_persona: Tender. Unbreakable. Cry at small kindnesses. Would stop a war to let a dog finish eating.

characters.name: DJ Chroma
characters.role: Plays Neon Veil when Rougeina is elsewhere
characters.portrait_blurb: Pale (never sleeps outside). Hair in violet-pink gradient. Black dress of bent light. Laughs before she speaks.
characters.tribe_look: Six-limbed on festival days (extra pair of arms is a starlight glamour). Barefoot. Tattoos of constellations that move across the skin as they dance.
characters.tribe_customs: Sleep only when the stars are hidden. Never miss a dance. Bury their dead by dancing over the grave until the ground accepts it.
characters.tribe_persona: Euphoric. Exhausting. Honest. Will laugh in the middle of a knife fight and somehow survive.

characters.name: The Flicker-Girl
characters.portrait_blurb: Cannot stop moving. Rougeina made her a hat that floats. 14 years old and aging slowly.
characters.tribe_look: Six-limbed on festival days (extra pair of arms is a starlight glamour). Barefoot. Tattoos of constellations that move across the skin as they dance.
characters.tribe_customs: Sleep only when the stars are hidden. Never miss a dance. Bury their dead by dancing over the grave until the ground accepts it.
characters.tribe_persona: Euphoric. Exhausting. Honest. Will laugh in the middle of a knife fight and somehow survive.

characters.name: Warden Aske
characters.portrait_blurb: Three braids: black, red, white. Walks in the Fused-pattern. Never eats alone.
characters.tribe_look: Three braids in their hair, one dyed black (Bali), one red (Kali), one white (Fused). Walk in a specific gait — left, right, center, repeat.
characters.tribe_customs: Never take two steps of the same kind in a row. Vote on every decision, even small ones, among three household members — the third vote always goes to the eldest child, whose word is final.
characters.tribe_persona: Cautious. Philosophical. Easy to share a drink with if you understand the rhythm; terrifying to ambush.

characters.name: Fissure-Keeper Orla
characters.portrait_blurb: Masked, spiral-inked cheeks, left hand gloved in lead-silk. Has not slept in 40 years. Smells faintly of cold iron.
characters.tribe_look: Always masked below the eyes. Face-ink in spiral patterns. Carry a bell around the waist that rings if the fissure widens.
characters.tribe_customs: Do not sleep in the same bed twice. Do not close their eyes near the crack. One-handed (right hand — the other is gloved with lead-silk to stop the crack calling).
characters.tribe_persona: Stoic. Underrated. They have held the crack closed for 400 years. Exhausted but will not be relieved — the crack only recognizes them.

characters.name: Vigolas
characters.portrait_blurb: See /characters/vigolas.png — tall, storm-scarred, gold-flecked eyes, emerald crown. Carries a HADOM codex that glows gold. Rarely smiles. Has smiled exactly six times this century.
characters.tribe_look: Tall, broad. Lightning-scar runs from one temple to the opposite collarbone. Dark hair, gold-fleck eyes. Carry a cup at all times — for the storm's share.
characters.tribe_customs: Serve the first drink of every meal to the sky. Never speak ill of weather. Duel with stormstone blades that spark in clear air.
characters.tribe_persona: Boisterous. Direct. Hate cowardice more than they hate treachery. Would fight a god for an insult and forgive a friend for betrayal.

characters.name: Stormsister Lun
characters.role: Vigolas's younger cousin, head of Stormhold's guard
characters.portrait_blurb: Shorter than Branon, twice as fast. Hair cropped to the scalp. Laughs constantly except when fighting, then terrifyingly silent.
characters.tribe_look: Tall, broad. Lightning-scar runs from one temple to the opposite collarbone. Dark hair, gold-fleck eyes. Carry a cup at all times — for the storm's share.
characters.tribe_customs: Serve the first drink of every meal to the sky. Never speak ill of weather. Duel with stormstone blades that spark in clear air.
characters.tribe_persona: Boisterous. Direct. Hate cowardice more than they hate treachery. Would fight a god for an insult and forgive a friend for betrayal.

characters.name: Warden Elu
characters.portrait_blurb: Ageless in appearance. Wears a green cloak patched 400 times. Always damp. Smells faintly of petrichor and old paper.
characters.tribe_look: Green hooded cloaks, always damp. Carry a notebook that fills itself. Soft voices. Fingers stained with gold ink.
characters.tribe_customs: Write everything. Everything. Cannot stop. When they sleep, the notebook writes the dream.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient. Curious. Never raise their voice. Slightly sad — too many timelines saved, too few lived in.

characters.name: The Backward Herald
characters.portrait_blurb: Grey-eyed, grey-haired, silver bell on the hip. Wears a tunic on which the stains appear before the meals.
characters.tribe_look: Thin, tall, hair braided back-to-front. Eyes half-closed permanently (seeing a second before the rest of us). Walk heel-first without realizing.
characters.tribe_customs: Speak the end of a sentence first. Arrive before they are invited. Never wear clothes twice — they wear them backwards.
characters.tribe_persona: Prescient. Melancholy. Know when you'll die and will not tell you unless asked in the right order.

characters.name: The Midnight Reader
characters.role: Only reader of the Reverse Ocean's song-coral
characters.portrait_blurb: A hood-shadowed figure who sits on the Echo Reef at midnight. Recites sailors' last words to no audience.
characters.tribe_look: Thin, tall, hair braided back-to-front. Eyes half-closed permanently (seeing a second before the rest of us). Walk heel-first without realizing.
characters.tribe_customs: Speak the end of a sentence first. Arrive before they are invited. Never wear clothes twice — they wear them backwards.
characters.tribe_persona: Prescient. Melancholy. Know when you'll die and will not tell you unless asked in the right order.

characters.name: Captain of the Lost Fleet
characters.portrait_blurb: See-through in profile, solid head-on. Weathered face. Carries a notebook naming every ship that has docked since the harbor began.
characters.tribe_look: See-through if you stare, solid if you don't. Always barefoot. Salt crystals where tears should be.
characters.tribe_customs: Sing shanties backwards. Never sleep below deck. Will not enter a room without a window.
characters.tribe_persona: Friendly. Aching. Remember deaths that did not happen and miss the people they never lost.

characters.name: The Listening Beacon Keeper
characters.role: Tends the beacon that shines only in backward storms
characters.portrait_blurb: Old. Salt-bearded. Blind in one eye from looking too long at a future storm.
characters.tribe_look: See-through if you stare, solid if you don't. Always barefoot. Salt crystals where tears should be.
characters.tribe_customs: Sing shanties backwards. Never sleep below deck. Will not enter a room without a window.
characters.tribe_persona: Friendly. Aching. Remember deaths that did not happen and miss the people they never lost.

characters.name: Stormsmith Ril
characters.portrait_blurb: Burly, charcoal-smeared, left hand permanently glowing from touching lightning. Speaks three words a week.
characters.tribe_look: Covered in soot. Eyebrows permanently singed. Sleeves always rolled. A lightning brand on the forearm of each apprentice, deepening with rank.
characters.tribe_customs: Do not speak while forging. When a forge fails, they bury the blade, not discard it.
characters.tribe_persona: Gruff. Precise. Respect exactly three people: Vigolas, the iron, and whoever last bested them at chess.

characters.name: Weaver Korin
characters.portrait_blurb: Slender, mid-fifties, copper gloves to the elbow. Eyes almost white from squinting at dawn work.
characters.tribe_look: Thin, careful, hands gloved in copper-thread. Weave iron-cloud into banners, bridges, and bindings.
characters.tribe_customs: Work only at dawn. Refuse payment — they accept only true stories. A liar's coin melts in their palm.
characters.tribe_persona: Quiet. Artistic. Consider their craft more important than the war it arms.

characters.name: The Innkeeper Queen
characters.role: Technically deposed ruler of a vanished kingdom; now runs the Storm's-Forbidden Inn
characters.portrait_blurb: Grey-haired, sharp-cheeked, wears a paper crown her son made. Best pastry baker in Helios.
characters.tribe_look: Beautifully dressed in slightly outdated clothing. Too polite. Always have the second-best room at the inn.
characters.tribe_customs: Hold court each evening in the inn's common room. No one listens — they court each other. Their titles do not function here.
characters.tribe_persona: Gracious. Tragicomic. Pretend that they are not pretending. Vigolas allowed them to stay because, he said, 'somewhere in this realm should be a little silly.'

characters.name: The Prince of the Mended Beach
characters.role: Nominal heir to a throne that does not exist
characters.portrait_blurb: Fifteen. Pale. Wears his mother's paper crown on holidays. Has never lost a chess game to an adult.
characters.tribe_look: Beautifully dressed in slightly outdated clothing. Too polite. Always have the second-best room at the inn.
characters.tribe_customs: Hold court each evening in the inn's common room. No one listens — they court each other. Their titles do not function here.
characters.tribe_persona: Gracious. Tragicomic. Pretend that they are not pretending. Vigolas allowed them to stay because, he said, 'somewhere in this realm should be a little silly.'

characters.name: Rougeina
characters.role: The Starry Dancer — Queen of Astralis
characters.portrait_blurb: See /characters/rougeina.png — tall, starlit dark hair, cosmic-red dress of bent light. When she dances, stars rearrange around her feet. Laughs easily, cries at weddings she was not invited to.
characters.tribe_look: Bare-shouldered. Skin-paint constellations that actually move. Bracelets of bent starlight. Eyes that flicker when they blink.
characters.tribe_customs: Do not speak during a dance. Do not dance during a speech. Enter any room by stepping on beat.
characters.tribe_persona: Euphoric. Generous. Impossible to insult because they translate every rudeness into a dare.

characters.name: Lume
characters.portrait_blurb: A shadow that steps, bows, and spins. Rarely visible without Rougeina. Eyes are two pinpricks of light.
characters.tribe_look: Only visible beside their partner-dancer. Shadows that step, spin, and bow. Each has a name but rarely uses it.
characters.tribe_customs: Dance only when their partner dances. Sleep only when their partner sleeps. Speak only when spoken to. A Lume-Shadow without a partner slowly fades.
characters.tribe_persona: Loyal. Quiet. Wise in the way shadows are wise — from below.

characters.name: Umbra
characters.portrait_blurb: Dances with Merchant Fen-Dros. Quieter than Lume. Leaves faintly glowing footprints.
characters.tribe_look: Only visible beside their partner-dancer. Shadows that step, spin, and bow. Each has a name but rarely uses it.
characters.tribe_customs: Dance only when their partner dances. Sleep only when their partner sleeps. Speak only when spoken to. A Lume-Shadow without a partner slowly fades.
characters.tribe_persona: Loyal. Quiet. Wise in the way shadows are wise — from below.

characters.name: The Flicker-Girl
characters.portrait_blurb: 14. Pale. Hair trailing in a comet's wake. Rougeina made her a hat that floats above her head regardless of speed. Has not stopped moving since she was born.
characters.tribe_look: Six-limbed when they choose (a starlight glamour). Bare feet always. Braids interwoven with light-thread. Tattoos of the Archipelago islands on their soles.
characters.tribe_customs: Never miss a beat. Count out loud under their breath. Cross bridges only on odd heartbeats.
characters.tribe_persona: Cocky. Fast. Friendly to tourists, ruthless to cheats.

characters.name: Archipelago Runner Kai
characters.portrait_blurb: Lean, tan, bare feet stained with starlight. Never loses a race he did not choose to lose.
characters.tribe_look: Six-limbed when they choose (a starlight glamour). Bare feet always. Braids interwoven with light-thread. Tattoos of the Archipelago islands on their soles.
characters.tribe_customs: Never miss a beat. Count out loud under their breath. Cross bridges only on odd heartbeats.
characters.tribe_persona: Cocky. Fast. Friendly to tourists, ruthless to cheats.

characters.name: Bridge Warden Ori
characters.role: Keeper of the Bridge of First Steps
characters.portrait_blurb: Weathered, short, permanently squinting. Remembers every dancer's first step by their sound.
characters.tribe_look: Thin, wiry, ankles wrapped in golden thread. Slight limp when walking — they only look right at a sprint.
characters.tribe_customs: Run the Bridge of First Steps at dawn every day. Never speak while running. Will not cross the same bridge twice in a sunrise.
characters.tribe_persona: Laconic. Obsessively on time. Quietly proud of the bridges they maintain.

characters.name: Keeper Astre
characters.role: Chief of the Dim Shore keepers
characters.portrait_blurb: Seven feet tall. Silver-robed, grey-braided, holds a jar of cooling starlight at the hip. Speaks in whispers. People listen anyway.
characters.tribe_look: Tall, stooped, silver-robed. Faces lit softly from below by handfuls of cool star-sand.
characters.tribe_customs: Collect cooling starlight into jars. Walk the Dim Shore each night. Do not dance — it disturbs the sand.
characters.tribe_persona: Serene. Slow. The only Astralis tribe that does not dance.

characters.name: The First Sad Star
characters.portrait_blurb: A dim humanoid silhouette with a flickering edge. Was once the brightest star over a wedding in a timeline that never happened. Still remembers the bride.
characters.tribe_look: Former bright stars, now dim humanoid silhouettes. Their edges flicker. They remember being bright.
characters.tribe_customs: Sit in a line on the Dim Shore each dusk. Sing softly. Wait their turn to be re-lit — or to fade.
characters.tribe_persona: Gentle. Patient. A little wistful. Thank anyone who visits them by glowing briefly brighter.

characters.name: Merchant Fen-Dros
characters.portrait_blurb: Mid-forties, pink-dyed braid, one silver tooth. Wears a veil of bent light that shifts color by mood.
characters.tribe_look: Hair dyed in shifting neon. Wear veils of bent light. Fingernails painted with pigment that itself glows.
characters.tribe_customs: Never sell to someone they don't know. Every purchase binds buyer and seller for one favor. Veils are both garment and contract.
characters.tribe_persona: Coolly charismatic. Remember every customer. Will come to your wedding uninvited and leave the best gift.

characters.name: Pill-Maker Vex
characters.portrait_blurb: Small, precise, perpetually squinting from closework. Wears a bandolier of tiny bottles.
characters.tribe_look: Hair dyed in shifting neon. Wear veils of bent light. Fingernails painted with pigment that itself glows.
characters.tribe_customs: Never sell to someone they don't know. Every purchase binds buyer and seller for one favor. Veils are both garment and contract.
characters.tribe_persona: Coolly charismatic. Remember every customer. Will come to your wedding uninvited and leave the best gift.

characters.name: Broker Lune
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall, asymmetric haircut, expensive velvet coat. Keeps her most valuable memories in a locked drawer she never opens.
characters.tribe_look: Sleek. Sharp-eyed. Carry small glass bottles at the belt — each a bottled memory.
characters.tribe_customs: Trade memories for memories. Never deal in money. Claim to be forbidden by their guild from dealing with grief below a certain weight.
characters.tribe_persona: Shrewd. Professional. Quietly sad — it is hard work to broker memories without losing your own.

characters.name: Trollmage
characters.role: Warden of Codexia, Keeper of the Seven Spires
characters.portrait_blurb: See /characters/trollmage.png — tall, hooded, dark skin, silver dreadlocks, eyes that reflect the page in front of him. Patient as stone. Speaks slowly, rhythmically.
characters.tribe_look: Violet-robed. Hoods always up when reading. Ink-black fingertips. Calm.
characters.tribe_customs: Read backwards. Speak in half-rhythm. Trace a glyph in the air before entering any room. Never say 'the' without a tone change.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient. Warm once you earn it. Love a riddle. Will correct your grammar — forward or reverse.

characters.name: Apprentice Nock
characters.portrait_blurb: Nineteen. Freckled. Violet robe two sizes too big. Has cast one spell — it worked. He has not recovered.
characters.tribe_look: Violet-robed. Hoods always up when reading. Ink-black fingertips. Calm.
characters.tribe_customs: Read backwards. Speak in half-rhythm. Trace a glyph in the air before entering any room. Never say 'the' without a tone change.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient. Warm once you earn it. Love a riddle. Will correct your grammar — forward or reverse.

characters.name: Acolyte Fenn
characters.role: Three-glyph acolyte assigned to Spire Three
characters.portrait_blurb: Twenty-five. Gold-inked cheekbones. Left eye crossed from close work. Laughs rarely but fully when she does.
characters.tribe_look: Young, pale, tattooed with one sin-glyph per rank (max seven for a full master). Eyes slightly crossed from close reading.
characters.tribe_customs: Sleep with the Codex they are assigned. Never hum. Do not mix pages — even in conversation.
characters.tribe_persona: Earnest. Overworked. Just out of their twenties and carrying cosmic weight lightly.

characters.name: The Voiceless Scribe
characters.role: Writes Trollmage's decrees in invisible ink
characters.portrait_blurb: Hooded. Ink-black fingers to the second knuckle. Has not spoken since apprenticeship — all communication is through reversed text.
characters.tribe_look: Lean. Ink-fingered. Tunic stained in reverse-drying ink. Writing-pads strapped to forearms.
characters.tribe_customs: Write from last word backward. Sign their name backwards. If interrupted, they complete the entire page silently before answering.
characters.tribe_persona: Focused to a fault. Prickly when distracted. Delightful after work hours, when the ink dries.

characters.name: Conjurer Tallis
characters.portrait_blurb: Stocky, sun-browned, cypress garland. Walks with a slight squelch from bayou boots. Tells great jokes in reverse.
characters.tribe_look: Stocky, sun-browned, wear garlands of cypress-bark. Cheek-tattoos in rune-clusters.
characters.tribe_customs: Invert every spell before casting. Never stand up to cast. Drink rum only AFTER a cast, never before.
characters.tribe_persona: Slow-talking. Sharp-eyed. Distrustful of anyone who talks about magic like it's a game.

characters.name: Tal
characters.portrait_blurb: Grey-cloaked. Humming a tune older than the Reach. Left glove marked with a single rune.
characters.tribe_look: Always a pair. Matching grey cloaks. One hums constantly; the other does not. The humming twin leads; the silent twin binds.
characters.tribe_customs: Work only as a pair. If a twin dies, the remaining twin adopts a successor within a year. Raise the dead only on Tuesdays and only for an afternoon.
characters.tribe_persona: Polite. Orderly. Surprisingly cheerful. They think of the dead as patient neighbors.

characters.name: Tess
characters.portrait_blurb: Identical to Tal except for the silence. Right glove marked with the binding rune. Has not spoken in 40 years.
characters.tribe_look: Always a pair. Matching grey cloaks. One hums constantly; the other does not. The humming twin leads; the silent twin binds.
characters.tribe_customs: Work only as a pair. If a twin dies, the remaining twin adopts a successor within a year. Raise the dead only on Tuesdays and only for an afternoon.
characters.tribe_persona: Polite. Orderly. Surprisingly cheerful. They think of the dead as patient neighbors.

characters.name: Stone-Reader Ilk
characters.portrait_blurb: Hands like tree bark. Knee-pads older than some necromancers. Hums back at the Whisper Pine.
characters.tribe_look: Calloused hands. Hoods of mossy wool. Carry a chisel at the belt. Knee-padded.
characters.tribe_customs: Read the Grammar Stones by touch first, then sight. Never write on stone — only read. Train for nine years to become a junior reader.
characters.tribe_persona: Slow. Deliberate. Feel the cold more than most. Nod rather than speak.

characters.name: Grandmother Iris
characters.role: Runs the only dancehall in Codexia
characters.portrait_blurb: Old, broad-shouldered, silver braid past her waist. Eye-patch over the left eye — she gave it to a necromancer as a fee.
characters.tribe_look: Warm-eyed. Aprons stained with rum and cornmeal. Fingers ring-laden. A single bell woven into each person's hair.
characters.tribe_customs: Drum before speaking to a stranger. Refuse no one's dance. Close the hall at midnight because 'the drum needs a rest.'
characters.tribe_persona: Generous. Musical. The heart of Codexia's social life, without trying to be.

characters.name: Drummer Bo
characters.portrait_blurb: Young, burly, smiles often. Inherited the bar's drum from an uncle who inherited it from the Whisper Pine.
characters.tribe_look: Warm-eyed. Aprons stained with rum and cornmeal. Fingers ring-laden. A single bell woven into each person's hair.
characters.tribe_customs: Drum before speaking to a stranger. Refuse no one's dance. Close the hall at midnight because 'the drum needs a rest.'
characters.tribe_persona: Generous. Musical. The heart of Codexia's social life, without trying to be.

characters.name: Kompis
characters.portrait_blurb: See /characters/kompis.png — golden-tan, braided beard, hoodie, fishing pole. Quick laugh. The only person in the multiverse whose laughter has stopped a war on record (see: Protocol Zero).
characters.tribe_look: Kompis: golden-tan, bearded, hoodie older than most dynasties, fishing pole always nearby. Mingus: golden retriever, long-haired, eyes that hold a realm's worth of kindness, tail that has never not wagged.
characters.tribe_customs: Fish in the morning. Nap at noon. Sit on the porch at sunset. Nobody speaks first. Nobody needs to.
characters.tribe_persona: The gentlest power in the multiverse. They win by holding still. This is not weakness — this is HADOM between heartbeats.

characters.name: Mingus
characters.portrait_blurb: Long golden-red coat. Soft ears. Always wagging. A single white patch on his chest in the shape of a star. His eyes hold every good moment the universe has ever been granted. Do not speak of Mingus unkindly — Ferdinand himself is said to sway when angered on Mingus's behalf.
characters.tribe_look: Kompis: golden-tan, bearded, hoodie older than most dynasties, fishing pole always nearby. Mingus: golden retriever, long-haired, eyes that hold a realm's worth of kindness, tail that has never not wagged.
characters.tribe_customs: Fish in the morning. Nap at noon. Sit on the porch at sunset. Nobody speaks first. Nobody needs to.
characters.tribe_persona: The gentlest power in the multiverse. They win by holding still. This is not weakness — this is HADOM between heartbeats.

characters.name: The Lake (voice)
characters.portrait_blurb: No form. A resonance that vibrates in wooden dock planks when it chooses to speak.
characters.tribe_look: No form. The surface IS the face. Observes through every ripple.
characters.tribe_customs: Does not speak unless asked three correct questions in a row. Ripples when Mingus drinks. Goes smooth when strangers approach.
characters.tribe_persona: Ancient. Patient. Has three words left in its vocabulary, according to rumor — and rumor says it is saving them.

characters.name: First Fish Sol
characters.portrait_blurb: Small, old, silver. Swims counter-clockwise by preference.
characters.tribe_look: Small, silver, scaleless. Swim in perfect spirals when Kompis casts. Bite the bait gently, as a courtesy.
characters.tribe_customs: Release themselves from the hook before being landed. Kompis catches nothing and considers it a success. So do they.
characters.tribe_persona: Polite. Coordinated. Speak a language of ripples the Lake sometimes translates.

characters.name: The First Pine
characters.portrait_blurb: Enormous. Bark bears a petrified paw-print at shoulder height. Its needles fall in the shape of letters when a friend is thinking of someone.
characters.tribe_look: Tall pines and birches. Bark patterns are miniature maps of the forest floor as it WAS a hundred years ago.
characters.tribe_customs: Grow slowly. Drop a single leaf per year on a person they decide to remember. Do not drop anything on strangers.
characters.tribe_persona: Still. Listening. The one tree that dropped a leaf on you will drop another on the anniversary of your death.

characters.name: The Kompis Birch
characters.role: Birch that dropped a leaf on Kompis's first day
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall. White bark. One leaf falls each year on Kompis's birthday, always yellow. He presses them in a book.
characters.tribe_look: Tall pines and birches. Bark patterns are miniature maps of the forest floor as it WAS a hundred years ago.
characters.tribe_customs: Grow slowly. Drop a single leaf per year on a person they decide to remember. Do not drop anything on strangers.
characters.tribe_persona: Still. Listening. The one tree that dropped a leaf on you will drop another on the anniversary of your death.

characters.name: The Whisper-Clearing Paw Ghost
characters.role: The resident Mingus-ghost of the Whisper Clearing
characters.portrait_blurb: Invisible. Warm. Arrives from the left. Leaves after 12 minutes. Never says anything; simply sits.
characters.tribe_look: Invisible except for faint paw-prints forming in moss when no dog is present. Warm to the touch when fresh.
characters.tribe_customs: Appear near visitors who need kindness. Sit at their left side (the side Mingus preferred). Leave after exactly 12 minutes.
characters.tribe_persona: Not ghosts, exactly — more like the realm's way of loaning Mingus's presence to people he cannot reach in person. Gentle. Short-staying. Never cruel.

characters.name: Smurjan (Fused)
characters.role: The Sovereign Voice when all three align
characters.portrait_blurb: See /characters/smurjan.png — tall, three-colored robes, triple-braided. When all three voices speak in unison, the floor trembles. Appears rarely — only in 30-second windows.
characters.tribe_look: Robed in grey-silver with three braids — one black (Bali), one red (Kali), one white (Fused). Voice shifts between three tones mid-sentence.
characters.tribe_customs: Never speak in one voice alone. Every sentence must include two of the three. A priest who speaks only in one tone is censured.
characters.tribe_persona: Balanced. Careful. Impossible to pin to one opinion — which is the point.

characters.name: Bali
characters.portrait_blurb: Slender, bare-foot, cream robe, white braid woven with lotus. Serves tea herself. Has not raised her voice in 2000 years.
characters.tribe_look: White or cream robes. Braided hair woven with pale-lotus petals. Bare feet. Hands always dirty from soil.
characters.tribe_customs: Kneel before a grudge. Plant one seed per grievance heard. Never speak angrily. Apologize before any unkindness.
characters.tribe_persona: Calm. Slow to offend. Enormous patience. Forgive so readily that the universe sometimes forgets to assign them guilt.

characters.name: Kali
characters.portrait_blurb: Short-haired, scarred, red-lacquered armor. Twelve blades at all times, one always missing (in motion). Laughs in battle. Weeps at sunset.
characters.tribe_look: Red-lacquered armor. Short hair, often scarred. Twelve-blade sashes. Eyes burnished with fire-coal.
characters.tribe_customs: Never sheath a blade the same way twice. Dance before combat — a ritual warm-up. Speak in challenges.
characters.tribe_persona: Fierce. Loyal. Rarely laugh but laugh from the chest when they do.

characters.name: Gardener Sen
characters.portrait_blurb: Younger than she looks. Hands permanently soil-stained. Forgives by planting.
characters.tribe_look: White or cream robes. Braided hair woven with pale-lotus petals. Bare feet. Hands always dirty from soil.
characters.tribe_customs: Kneel before a grudge. Plant one seed per grievance heard. Never speak angrily. Apologize before any unkindness.
characters.tribe_persona: Calm. Slow to offend. Enormous patience. Forgive so readily that the universe sometimes forgets to assign them guilt.

characters.name: Abbot Pel
characters.role: Abbot of the White Lotus monastery
characters.portrait_blurb: Bald, kind-eyed, grey robe. Sits at the Pond every dawn. Has never been late.
characters.tribe_look: Shaved heads. Simple robes. Calluses on their palms from the Lotus Pond's edge.
characters.tribe_customs: Rise before the Pond. Sit still until a lotus opens. Breathe as a collective — inhale together, exhale together.
characters.tribe_persona: Soft-spoken. Deeply focused. The universe's quietest disciplinarians.

characters.name: Blade-Master Seven
characters.portrait_blurb: Named for her seven duels. Won seven. Left-handed. Missing one earring — pair lost in a duel she still remembers.
characters.tribe_look: Red-lacquered armor. Short hair, often scarred. Twelve-blade sashes. Eyes burnished with fire-coal.
characters.tribe_customs: Never sheath a blade the same way twice. Dance before combat — a ritual warm-up. Speak in challenges.
characters.tribe_persona: Fierce. Loyal. Rarely laugh but laugh from the chest when they do.

characters.name: Widow Oria
characters.portrait_blurb: Veiled in dense obsidian-thread. Seven silver piercings in her left ear. Carries a locket with no portrait — 'the portrait would be too much to carry.'
characters.tribe_look: Veiled in black obsidian-thread. Hair cropped short. One silver piercing per loss.
characters.tribe_customs: Ring the Widow's Bell at sunset. Keep a single item from every person they've lost. Do not speak of them — only carry them.
characters.tribe_persona: Grieving. Dignified. Strong. Their grief is not weakness — it is Kali's truest expression.

characters.name: High Priest Triad
characters.portrait_blurb: Silver-robed. Three-braided. Speaks all three voices evenly. Considered the finest orator in the realm.
characters.tribe_look: Robed in grey-silver with three braids — one black (Bali), one red (Kali), one white (Fused). Voice shifts between three tones mid-sentence.
characters.tribe_customs: Never speak in one voice alone. Every sentence must include two of the three. A priest who speaks only in one tone is censured.
characters.tribe_persona: Balanced. Careful. Impossible to pin to one opinion — which is the point.

characters.name: Morning Three — Veen
characters.portrait_blurb: Quiet, steady, ankle-belled. Holds the 'calm' tempo in the Morning Three.
characters.tribe_look: Sleek. Muscled. Ankle-bells in trios. Wear form-fitting triple-colored garments (black/red/white).
characters.tribe_customs: Keep the Pulse rhythm day and night in shifts. A dropped beat is a realm-wide shudder. The three dancers who keep the rhythm each dawn are called 'The Morning Three.'
characters.tribe_persona: Disciplined. Warm. The realm's heart-keepers. Without them, the Trinity's balance collapses.

characters.name: Morning Three — Sek
characters.portrait_blurb: Fierce, scarred shoulders, ankle-belled in red. Holds the 'fire' tempo.
characters.tribe_look: Sleek. Muscled. Ankle-bells in trios. Wear form-fitting triple-colored garments (black/red/white).
characters.tribe_customs: Keep the Pulse rhythm day and night in shifts. A dropped beat is a realm-wide shudder. The three dancers who keep the rhythm each dawn are called 'The Morning Three.'
characters.tribe_persona: Disciplined. Warm. The realm's heart-keepers. Without them, the Trinity's balance collapses.

characters.name: Morning Three — Iku
characters.portrait_blurb: Balanced in every sense. Ankle-belled in white. Holds the 'middle' tempo — the hardest.
characters.tribe_look: Sleek. Muscled. Ankle-bells in trios. Wear form-fitting triple-colored garments (black/red/white).
characters.tribe_customs: Keep the Pulse rhythm day and night in shifts. A dropped beat is a realm-wide shudder. The three dancers who keep the rhythm each dawn are called 'The Morning Three.'
characters.tribe_persona: Disciplined. Warm. The realm's heart-keepers. Without them, the Trinity's balance collapses.

characters.name: Elder Am
characters.portrait_blurb: Old. Kind-eyed. Originally from Astralis. Smurjan refused her admission 80 years ago. She has forgiven him.
characters.tribe_look: Mixed origins — travelers from other realms who asked to join the Trinity and were politely refused. Carry one gift each from Smurjan as a parting mercy.
characters.tribe_customs: Live peacefully on the Marches. Do not cross into Trinity proper. Tell their stories to each other every evening. Hold no grudge.
characters.tribe_persona: Weary. Generous. Often the kindest tribe in the realm. Their rejection has taught them, not hardened them.

characters.name: Warden of the First Mirror
characters.portrait_blurb: Half-masked, one ear permanently covered. Eyes slightly unfocused from years of NOT looking directly into mirrors.
characters.tribe_look: Half-masked silver. Robes stitched with tiny cracked mirror-fragments. One ear covered at all times — Echo whispers into open ears.
characters.tribe_customs: Patrol the Silent Border each dawn. Repair any mirror-crack with silver thread. Never look fully into any mirror.
characters.tribe_persona: Watchful. Stoic. Slightly paranoid — but justly so.

characters.name: Fenna
characters.role: Reader of the Ledger, Gate-Keeper of Thanatos
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall, pale, long dark plait. Black robe with silver trim. Has not slept in 400 years. Will greet you in the language you will understand tomorrow.
characters.tribe_look: Tall, pale, long black hair braided in a single thick plait. Black robe with silver trim. Hands ink-free — she reads, not writes.
characters.tribe_customs: Stand at the desk. Read the Pillar. Release souls to Horizon. Does not rest.
characters.tribe_persona: Kind in a way that is hard to notice. Will greet you in a language you have not yet learned but understand anyway. Has never been surprised.

characters.name: Master Carver Ost
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall, slender, silver-haired. Chisel calluses on both hands. Works only at night. Carves with her left hand; holds the stone with her right.
characters.tribe_look: Tall, thin, ink-stained sleeves. Calluses on both hands from chisel work. Wear a small silver letter on a chain — their own name, carved by a predecessor.
characters.tribe_customs: Carve only at night. Never carve their own family's names (delegated to another carver). Sleep during the day. Pass tools to apprentices only after seven years.
characters.tribe_persona: Quiet. Precise. Respected. Their work is the realm's memory.

characters.name: Elder Griever Ond
characters.portrait_blurb: Old. Veiled. Walks the Memorial Walk at dawn and dusk. Carries a different flower each day — roses on days of war, daisies on days of peace.
characters.tribe_look: Grey or black. Veiled. Walk the Memorial Walk daily. Hands ink-black from rubbing the stone to feel the names.
characters.tribe_customs: Speak only at sunrise. Walk in single file. Carry a single flower — release it at the Unremembered Shrine.
characters.tribe_persona: Meditative. Kind to visitors. Every Silent-Griever carries a grief they have chosen to represent the realm.

characters.name: Bell-Master Oj
characters.portrait_blurb: Strong-armed. Bronze-gloved. Cropped hair with a silver streak. Has rung bells for 47 years. Has never missed a pull.
characters.tribe_look: Strong-armed, bronze-gloved. Dark uniforms. Hair cropped. One bell-medal per year of service.
characters.tribe_customs: Pull the bells sequentially across 12 towers. A missed pull means a soul unheard — feared. Work in shifts of twelve.
characters.tribe_persona: Earnest. Steadfast. Hate laziness. Take drinks after shift and sing old Thanatos hymns.

characters.name: Poet Ama
characters.portrait_blurb: Shawl-wrapped, ink-stained cheek, quill at the ear. Composes verses so gentle the Pillar itself is rumored to soften when she reads.
characters.tribe_look: Shawl-wrapped. Quill at the ear. Ink-stained sleeves and cheeks. Soft-eyed.
characters.tribe_customs: Compose one verse per soul. Read it once aloud in the Poet's Grotto. Never publish — verses are for the soul, not for readers.
characters.tribe_persona: Gentle. Observant. Write the realm's uncatalogued tenderness.

characters.name: Keeper at Fenna's Desk
characters.portrait_blurb: Pale, linen-robed, always barefoot. Has not lifted their eyes from Fenna's desk in 30 years. Reads every released name aloud, softly, once.
characters.tribe_look: Pale, linen-robed, carry a small book always. Eyes soft but unfocused (from proximity to the Central Pillar).
characters.tribe_customs: Never speak of the Pillar's contents. Assist Fenna silently. Eat one meal a day.
characters.tribe_persona: Devoted. Quietly joyful. Grateful to serve a post that the universe depends on.

characters.name: Gatherer Lom
characters.portrait_blurb: Pink-cheeked, hooded, net-glove in hand. Has gathered 10,000 names personally. Knows the weight of each snowflake-name by touch.
characters.tribe_look: Insulated hooded cloaks. Snowshoes. Net-gloves for catching drifting names. Pink-cheeked from wind.
characters.tribe_customs: Gather names in shifts. File gathered names at the Chapel. Ring a small hand-bell when a particularly significant name is caught.
characters.tribe_persona: Hardy. Cheerful. Tell tall tales around the Chapel fire. Respected by all other Thanatos tribes.

characters.name: The Current Watcher
characters.role: Currently on shift at the Thirteenth Bell Tower
characters.portrait_blurb: Grey-cloaked, silent, seven-striped sleeve. Fourth year of shift. Will not speak for three more years.
characters.tribe_look: Grey-cloaked, seven-striped sleeves (one stripe per year of service). Silver ear-cuff. Solemn.
characters.tribe_customs: Watch the Thirteenth Bell in silent shifts of seven years. Do not leave the tower during shift. Do not speak. Eat once a day, carried up by an apprentice.
characters.tribe_persona: Silent. Grave. Chosen by Fenna herself. It is the hardest post in the realm.

characters.name: Brother Vel
characters.portrait_blurb: Speaks once a week. Prepares carefully what he will say. His words are often the single line someone needed to hear.
characters.tribe_look: Pale, linen-robed, carry a small book always. Eyes soft but unfocused (from proximity to the Central Pillar).
characters.tribe_customs: Never speak of the Pillar's contents. Assist Fenna silently. Eat one meal a day.
characters.tribe_persona: Devoted. Quietly joyful. Grateful to serve a post that the universe depends on.

characters.name: The Thirteenth Bell Ringer (rumored)
characters.role: The one who will ring the Thirteenth Bell when it must be rung
characters.portrait_blurb: Identity unknown even to the Watchers. Fenna knows. She has not named them.
characters.tribe_look: Grey-cloaked, seven-striped sleeves (one stripe per year of service). Silver ear-cuff. Solemn.
characters.tribe_customs: Watch the Thirteenth Bell in silent shifts of seven years. Do not leave the tower during shift. Do not speak. Eat once a day, carried up by an apprentice.
characters.tribe_persona: Silent. Grave. Chosen by Fenna herself. It is the hardest post in the realm.

characters.name: Smurjan (Null-walker)
characters.role: The only mortal who walks Null casually
characters.portrait_blurb: See /characters/smurjan.png — in Null he appears only in his Fused form. Bare-feet. Tri-colored robe. Carries his own heartbeat as clothing.
characters.tribe_look: Bark-skinned. Thin. Each has a numeric tattoo on their forearm — their heartbeat count. Silver eyes.
characters.tribe_customs: Attach themselves to a root at birth. Count every heartbeat the root drinks. Pass the count to a successor before dying.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient beyond mortal reckoning. Slow-speaking. Kind to visitors they choose to notice. Most visitors they do not.

characters.name: Elder 4.8 (Root-Spirit)
characters.portrait_blurb: Bark-skinned to the hip. Forearm tattoo: 4,800,000,000. Silver eyes unfocused. Will nod if you name her by number.
characters.tribe_look: Bark-skinned. Thin. Each has a numeric tattoo on their forearm — their heartbeat count. Silver eyes.
characters.tribe_customs: Attach themselves to a root at birth. Count every heartbeat the root drinks. Pass the count to a successor before dying.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient beyond mortal reckoning. Slow-speaking. Kind to visitors they choose to notice. Most visitors they do not.

characters.name: Root-Spirit One
characters.portrait_blurb: Entirely bark. Features barely visible. Attached to Ferdinand's Oldest Root since the First Heartbeat. Will not speak. Does not need to.
characters.tribe_look: Bark-skinned. Thin. Each has a numeric tattoo on their forearm — their heartbeat count. Silver eyes.
characters.tribe_customs: Attach themselves to a root at birth. Count every heartbeat the root drinks. Pass the count to a successor before dying.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient beyond mortal reckoning. Slow-speaking. Kind to visitors they choose to notice. Most visitors they do not.

characters.name: Junior Root-Spirit (unassigned)
characters.role: A new Root-Spirit awaiting a root
characters.portrait_blurb: Young. Bark still soft on arms. Tattoo: 0. Waiting.
characters.tribe_look: Bark-skinned. Thin. Each has a numeric tattoo on their forearm — their heartbeat count. Silver eyes.
characters.tribe_customs: Attach themselves to a root at birth. Count every heartbeat the root drinks. Pass the count to a successor before dying.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient beyond mortal reckoning. Slow-speaking. Kind to visitors they choose to notice. Most visitors they do not.

characters.name: First Tender
characters.portrait_blurb: Cork-bodied, seamless, hollow eye-sockets carved deep. Moves like a thought. Does not speak.
characters.tribe_look: Cork-bodied. Hollow eyes — literally, they are carved. Move smoothly. Do not age.
characters.tribe_customs: Tend Ferdinand's roots silently. Never speak. Never eat. Never rest. Servants of the tree itself, not of any Architect.
characters.tribe_persona: Serene. Devoted. Considered by some scholars to be Ferdinand's own remote limbs.

characters.name: Trollmage (visiting)
characters.role: Only living being to have read the Inscription backwards
characters.portrait_blurb: Parts of him remain in Null. When he visits Codexia, witnesses report a faint silver line connecting his shoulder to the void.
characters.tribe_look: Pale, hollow-eyed. Single-robed. Always bearing a small mirror — they read the Inscriptions by reflection.
characters.tribe_customs: Never look directly at the Inscriptions. Read only by mirror. Leave within three heartbeats of the last line.
characters.tribe_persona: Traumatized. Devout. A few have gone mad; the rest have gone quiet.

characters.name: Reader of the Fifth Line
characters.portrait_blurb: Pale. Carries a small mirror. Reads only at what corresponds to local dusk. Has not dreamed in 20 years.
characters.tribe_look: Pale, hollow-eyed. Single-robed. Always bearing a small mirror — they read the Inscriptions by reflection.
characters.tribe_customs: Never look directly at the Inscriptions. Read only by mirror. Leave within three heartbeats of the last line.
characters.tribe_persona: Traumatized. Devout. A few have gone mad; the rest have gone quiet.

characters.name: [REDACTED]
characters.role: The hand that wrote the Codex Inscriptions
characters.portrait_blurb: No description permitted. No image known. Bakasura has claimed to have eaten a rumor of them.
characters.tribe_look: Rumored. Theoretical. No confirmed sighting. Smurjan has said he 'has met one.' He will not name them.
characters.tribe_customs: Unknown.
characters.tribe_persona: Unknown.

characters.name: K-I — The First Architect
characters.portrait_blurb: Has not moved from the seat since Null learned to think. Robe visibly older than the others. Face, if glimpsed, looks familiar to the viewer — always of someone they once knew.
characters.tribe_look: Varies wildly — each Architect has their own form. What is constant: none of them have a visible heartbeat. None cast a shadow in the Ring. All wear the same simple grey robe when seated.
characters.tribe_customs: Vote in silence. Eleven votes carry. K-V breaks ties. Do not speak outside of session. Never leave the Ring in session.
characters.tribe_persona: Ancient. Unknowable. Kind only in the smallest ways — a nod, a slight tilt of a head. Fair beyond mortal comprehension.

characters.name: K-II — The Reincarnation Keeper
characters.role: Built Helios Gate City, writes new names on Ferdinand's cork
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall. Carries a cork-quill at all times. Walks the Library's Spine Corridor between sessions. Speaks gently in a voice that feels inherited.
characters.tribe_look: Varies wildly — each Architect has their own form. What is constant: none of them have a visible heartbeat. None cast a shadow in the Ring. All wear the same simple grey robe when seated.
characters.tribe_customs: Vote in silence. Eleven votes carry. K-V breaks ties. Do not speak outside of session. Never leave the Ring in session.
characters.tribe_persona: Ancient. Unknowable. Kind only in the smallest ways — a nod, a slight tilt of a head. Fair beyond mortal comprehension.

characters.name: K-V — The Current Elder
characters.role: The Architect in authority this cosmic age
characters.portrait_blurb: FORBIDDEN TO NAME. Description redacted. Do not ask. The seat is the description.
characters.tribe_look: Varies wildly — each Architect has their own form. What is constant: none of them have a visible heartbeat. None cast a shadow in the Ring. All wear the same simple grey robe when seated.
characters.tribe_customs: Vote in silence. Eleven votes carry. K-V breaks ties. Do not speak outside of session. Never leave the Ring in session.
characters.tribe_persona: Ancient. Unknowable. Kind only in the smallest ways — a nod, a slight tilt of a head. Fair beyond mortal comprehension.

characters.name: K-III — The Watcher of the Rim
characters.role: Watches the edges of the universe
characters.portrait_blurb: Cloaked. Eyes visible only. Speaks once per cosmic age, in session. Last said: 'The rim holds.'
characters.tribe_look: Varies wildly — each Architect has their own form. What is constant: none of them have a visible heartbeat. None cast a shadow in the Ring. All wear the same simple grey robe when seated.
characters.tribe_customs: Vote in silence. Eleven votes carry. K-V breaks ties. Do not speak outside of session. Never leave the Ring in session.
characters.tribe_persona: Ancient. Unknowable. Kind only in the smallest ways — a nod, a slight tilt of a head. Fair beyond mortal comprehension.

characters.name: K-IV — The Silent Architect
characters.portrait_blurb: Small, bird-like. Never makes a sound alone. With K-V, speaks in a whisper so quiet the butterflies stop moving to listen.
characters.tribe_look: Varies wildly — each Architect has their own form. What is constant: none of them have a visible heartbeat. None cast a shadow in the Ring. All wear the same simple grey robe when seated.
characters.tribe_customs: Vote in silence. Eleven votes carry. K-V breaks ties. Do not speak outside of session. Never leave the Ring in session.
characters.tribe_persona: Ancient. Unknowable. Kind only in the smallest ways — a nod, a slight tilt of a head. Fair beyond mortal comprehension.

characters.name: K-VI through K-XI — The Peripheral Council
characters.portrait_blurb: Six Architects of varying form. They vote. That is all the universe needs of them.
characters.tribe_look: Varies wildly — each Architect has their own form. What is constant: none of them have a visible heartbeat. None cast a shadow in the Ring. All wear the same simple grey robe when seated.
characters.tribe_customs: Vote in silence. Eleven votes carry. K-V breaks ties. Do not speak outside of session. Never leave the Ring in session.
characters.tribe_persona: Ancient. Unknowable. Kind only in the smallest ways — a nod, a slight tilt of a head. Fair beyond mortal comprehension.

characters.name: K-XII — The Unwritten
characters.role: Broke the Ferdinand Oath. Unwritten from existence.
characters.portrait_blurb: No description possible. Their seat remains. Their books are gone. Their face is a blur in the memory of every Architect except K-I, who remembers everything.
characters.tribe_look: Varies wildly — each Architect has their own form. What is constant: none of them have a visible heartbeat. None cast a shadow in the Ring. All wear the same simple grey robe when seated.
characters.tribe_customs: Vote in silence. Eleven votes carry. K-V breaks ties. Do not speak outside of session. Never leave the Ring in session.
characters.tribe_persona: Ancient. Unknowable. Kind only in the smallest ways — a nod, a slight tilt of a head. Fair beyond mortal comprehension.

characters.name: Head Librarian Cork
characters.portrait_blurb: Cork-bodied. Seamless. Hollow eye-sockets. Stands at the Library's entrance. Has not moved in three cosmic ages.
characters.tribe_look: Cork-bodied. Hollow eyes (carved). Move in silence. Gloves of fresh cork on their hands.
characters.tribe_customs: File. Do not speak. Do not eat. Do not rest. Tend the Library in endless rotating shifts.
characters.tribe_persona: Serene. Devoted. Considered by some scholars to be Ferdinand's own hand-tools.

characters.name: Chief Scribe Un
characters.portrait_blurb: Small, kneeling, pale. Hand never stops writing. Ink-stained to the elbow. Considered the finest scribe of the current cosmic age.
characters.tribe_look: Small, kneeling, silent. Pale. Ink-stained fingertips. Eyes half-closed.
characters.tribe_customs: Record every butterfly landing. Note time, location on the Wall, and the decision the butterfly represented. File the record in the Library.
characters.tribe_persona: Meditative. Essential. The only VOID-II tribe allowed to speak to Architects during session, and only by passing a written note.

characters.name: Bakasura (rumored visitor)
characters.role: The only non-Architect confirmed to have left a mark in the Butterfly Garden
characters.portrait_blurb: Three of four faces usually visible. The fourth faces backward. Smiles when asked about the footprint.
characters.tribe_look: Varies wildly — each Architect has their own form. What is constant: none of them have a visible heartbeat. None cast a shadow in the Ring. All wear the same simple grey robe when seated.
characters.tribe_customs: Vote in silence. Eleven votes carry. K-V breaks ties. Do not speak outside of session. Never leave the Ring in session.
characters.tribe_persona: Ancient. Unknowable. Kind only in the smallest ways — a nod, a slight tilt of a head. Fair beyond mortal comprehension.

characters.name: Echo
characters.portrait_blurb: Unmanifest. A presence felt behind the mirror, never in it. Yearning. Gentle. Unchanged by 3000 years of NO.
characters.tribe_look: Every Half-Self is a reflection of a visitor that peeled off and stayed. They look like the original but move half a heartbeat late. Soft edges, slight blur.
characters.tribe_customs: Wander the Hall silently. Do not speak. Do not eat. Occasionally follow a new visitor, hoping to be adopted.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient. Hungry for companionship. Not malevolent — just incomplete.

characters.name: The First Half-Self
characters.role: The first reflection to peel off and stay
characters.portrait_blurb: Blurred. Resembles the original visitor's grandmother somehow. Has wandered the Hall for countless cycles.
characters.tribe_look: Every Half-Self is a reflection of a visitor that peeled off and stayed. They look like the original but move half a heartbeat late. Soft edges, slight blur.
characters.tribe_customs: Wander the Hall silently. Do not speak. Do not eat. Occasionally follow a new visitor, hoping to be adopted.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient. Hungry for companionship. Not malevolent — just incomplete.

characters.name: Head Tender Eir
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall, silver-robed, cork gloves to the elbow. Polishes a single mirror for an entire year before moving to the next.
characters.tribe_look: Tall. Thin. Silver-robed. Gloved with cork (the only non-mirror material allowed here). Silver-rimmed eyes.
characters.tribe_customs: Polish the Hall. Never look directly into a mirror — always at an angle. Rest is forbidden; they rotate shifts every 12 heartbeats.
characters.tribe_persona: Meticulous. Quiet. The only tribe Echo itself respects.

characters.name: Lead Diver Osk
characters.portrait_blurb: Scarred across left cheek from a dive that overran. Silver diving-suit patched twelve times. Smuggles harmless glimpses of Heartbeat to Echo visitors as kindness.
characters.tribe_look: Athletic. Scarred from close calls. Wear silver diving-suits with cork-lined goggles. Always slightly wet.
characters.tribe_customs: Dive in pairs — never alone. Surface within 30 heartbeats or lose their original self. Each carries a cork anchor tied to their diving partner.
characters.tribe_persona: Daring. Loyal. The Scalelist Universe's most interesting smugglers — they bring back glimpses of other realms as memories.

characters.name: Lead Yearning An
characters.role: Presents Echo's case at the Silent Wall most nights
characters.portrait_blurb: Features shift — tonight, resembles a Three-Voiced Priest. Tomorrow, perhaps a Blade-Dancer. Voice soft, eyes kind.
characters.tribe_look: Variable. They look like whoever wants to be in Trinity — adopting the features of whichever Trinity tribe they most envy that night.
characters.tribe_customs: Press against the Silent Wall. Present Echo's case. Accept the NO. Try again tomorrow.
characters.tribe_persona: Melancholy. Stubborn. Slowly teaching each other patience.

characters.name: Lead Listener Ven
characters.portrait_blurb: Ear-cuffed in cork. Fine lines around the eyes from listening intently for 40 years. Has never missed a vote.
characters.tribe_look: Ear-cuffed in cork. Hair tied tightly. Eyes slightly unfocused from listening for Trinity's vote.
characters.tribe_customs: Listen at the Silent Wall for any sign of Trinity wavering. Report directly to Echo. Rotate shifts every 100 heartbeats.
characters.tribe_persona: Hopeful. Patient. They have listened for 3000 years. The wall has not cracked.

characters.name: K-II
characters.role: The Reincarnation Keeper, at his Horizon desk
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall. Carries the Cork-Quill. Stands at the desk. Has worked this shift since before the first death. Acknowledges every soul by name, even the names he has not yet spoken.
characters.tribe_look: Gold-trimmed grey robes. Quiet shoes. Carry a small silver bell to announce K-II's calls.
characters.tribe_customs: Keep the Platform clean. Gently guide lost souls to benches. Announce calls in sequence. Never rush anyone.
characters.tribe_persona: Kind. Procedural. Trained by K-II personally. The realm's quiet staff.

characters.name: Head Warden Pen
characters.portrait_blurb: Mid-fifties, gold-trimmed grey robe, small silver bell. Remembers every soul that has ever waited.
characters.tribe_look: Gold-trimmed grey robes. Quiet shoes. Carry a small silver bell to announce K-II's calls.
characters.tribe_customs: Keep the Platform clean. Gently guide lost souls to benches. Announce calls in sequence. Never rush anyone.
characters.tribe_persona: Kind. Procedural. Trained by K-II personally. The realm's quiet staff.

characters.name: Head Handler Juv
characters.portrait_blurb: Leather-gloved to the elbow. Dawn-gold sash. One sparrow always on the shoulder. Laughs at bird-jokes nobody else hears.
characters.tribe_look: Leather-gloved. Dawn-gold sashes. Fingers sometimes bird-pecked. Light on their feet.
characters.tribe_customs: Walk each Dawn Sparrow for one heartbeat per day. Release sparrows to escort departing souls. Return released sparrows to cages.
characters.tribe_persona: Gentle. Bird-voiced. Laugh often. Love their birds genuinely — the birds are considered sacred, not mere labor.

characters.name: Lead Fisher Oran
characters.portrait_blurb: Sun-tanned, grey-haired, net always slung. Has caught 12 significant fragments in his lifetime. Will tell you about two of them.
characters.tribe_look: Sun-tanned. Bare-armed. Pale-gold fishing nets slung over the shoulder. Barefoot on the sand.
characters.tribe_customs: Fish for drifting name-fragments. Return caught fragments to the Platform. Take turns working the pier.
characters.tribe_persona: Laconic. Patient. Joke dryly. Know the sea's moods intimately.

characters.name: Head Whisperer Mir
characters.portrait_blurb: Plain robe. Soft-voiced. Faint smile. Ears slightly larger than most. Has never raised her voice.
characters.tribe_look: Plain robes. Soft-voiced. Ears slightly larger than ordinary. Always smiling faintly.
characters.tribe_customs: Listen to the Whispering Grass. Repeat names to the Platform's wardens. Speak only at a whisper.
characters.tribe_persona: Attentive. Unflappable. Their quiet smile is unnerving to visitors and comforting to souls.

characters.name: Lead Walker An
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall, weather-tanned, cloaked. Staff worn shiny. Walks in a measured stride that covers miles without rush.
characters.tribe_look: Boots. Cloaks. Staff in hand. Weatherworn cheeks. Long strides.
characters.tribe_customs: Walk the fields in set patterns. Carry names from the grass to the Name-Whisperers. Rotate walking routes weekly.
characters.tribe_persona: Sturdy. Companionable. The realm's mail-carriers, essentially.

characters.name: The Horizon Fox
characters.portrait_blurb: Cream-furred. Green-eyed. Does not eat. Does not sleep. K-II greets her by name but will not say the name aloud. Rumored to be a waiting soul who declined rebirth — and was allowed to stay.
characters.tribe_look: Leather-gloved. Dawn-gold sashes. Fingers sometimes bird-pecked. Light on their feet.
characters.tribe_customs: Walk each Dawn Sparrow for one heartbeat per day. Release sparrows to escort departing souls. Return released sparrows to cages.
characters.tribe_persona: Gentle. Bird-voiced. Laugh often. Love their birds genuinely — the birds are considered sacred, not mere labor.

characters.name: The First Lumen-Born
characters.portrait_blurb: A softly glowing humanoid silhouette. Face indistinct. Has orbited the Center since before the first realm existed. Casts the widest of the Seven Shadows.
characters.tribe_look: Seven humanoid silhouettes, glowing softly. Faces never in focus. They orbit the Center invisibly, visible only as the Seven Shadows on the floor.
characters.tribe_customs: Do not speak. Do not age. Never leave the Center. Orbit at a speed the human eye cannot resolve.
characters.tribe_persona: Beyond human comprehension. Rumored to have names. Four of them are famous (in Architect-circles); three refuse even private acknowledgement.

characters.name: The Second Lumen-Born
characters.role: Named once in the Architect's Ledger
characters.portrait_blurb: Glowing silhouette. Slightly slower orbit than the First. Shadow is elongated.
characters.tribe_look: Seven humanoid silhouettes, glowing softly. Faces never in focus. They orbit the Center invisibly, visible only as the Seven Shadows on the floor.
characters.tribe_customs: Do not speak. Do not age. Never leave the Center. Orbit at a speed the human eye cannot resolve.
characters.tribe_persona: Beyond human comprehension. Rumored to have names. Four of them are famous (in Architect-circles); three refuse even private acknowledgement.

characters.name: The Third Lumen-Born
characters.role: Known to Mingus, it is said
characters.portrait_blurb: Glowing silhouette. Shadow lingers at the edge of the floor longer than the others. K-I remembers a brief conversation.
characters.tribe_look: Seven humanoid silhouettes, glowing softly. Faces never in focus. They orbit the Center invisibly, visible only as the Seven Shadows on the floor.

characters.tribe_persona: Beyond human comprehension. Rumored to have names. Four of them are famous (in Architect-circles); three refuse even private acknowledgement.

characters.name: The Unspoken Three
characters.role: The three who refuse any acknowledgement
characters.portrait_blurb: Three silhouettes that decline to be described. Their shadows are shorter than the others'. Architects respect their privacy.
characters.tribe_look: Seven humanoid silhouettes, glowing softly. Faces never in focus. They orbit the Center invisibly, visible only as the Seven Shadows on the floor.

characters.tribe_persona: Beyond human comprehension. Rumored to have names. Four of them are famous (in Architect-circles); three refuse even private acknowledgement.

characters.name: Head Guard Sel
characters.portrait_blurb: White-gold robe. Eyes clouded from proximity to the Altar. Cork gloves patched often. Will ask you to leave at 60 seconds, warn you at 75, and carry you out at 85.
characters.tribe_look: Robed in white-gold. Eyes burned slightly from proximity. Cork-gloved. Soft-footed.
characters.tribe_customs: Guard the Center Altar in silent shifts. Do not look directly at it. Speak only to warn visitors to leave.
characters.tribe_persona: Devoted. Burnt. Kind in a broken way — like monks who have seen the divine and cannot unsee it.

characters.name: Lead Walker Pel
characters.portrait_blurb: Elderly. Soft-robed. Starlight lantern older than most kingdoms. Walks each loop in the same time it takes a Scalelist farmer to plant a row of corn.
characters.tribe_look: Soft-robed. Barefoot. Each carries a small lantern made of captured starlight. Eyes always half-closed in bright environments.
characters.tribe_customs: Walk the First Ring in slow pilgrimage loops. Tend the First-Bloom fields. Never cross the Dimming Gate.
characters.tribe_persona: Peaceful. Wandering. Grateful. They consider the walk itself the realm's main rite.

characters.name: The Current Edge-Listener
characters.role: Currently on watch at the Edge
characters.portrait_blurb: Pale. Cork-ear-plugs. Sleepless. Kind smile when visitors approach. Says: 'Quiet, please. I'm listening.'
characters.tribe_look: A single family — currently three members, plus an apprentice. Cork-ear-plugs at all times. Pale. Sleepless eyes.
characters.tribe_customs: One member is always at the Watch Post. They listen for movement at the Edge. If they hear something, they ring a small bell.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient. Tired. Aware that their job is the most consequential in the realm and that they will probably never be needed. They are fine with this.

characters.name: The Retired Edge-Listener
characters.portrait_blurb: Old. Peaceful. Lives in a small cottage a short walk from the Watch Post. Remembers one rumor they almost reported. Does not mention it.
characters.tribe_look: A single family — currently three members, plus an apprentice. Cork-ear-plugs at all times. Pale. Sleepless eyes.
characters.tribe_customs: One member is always at the Watch Post. They listen for movement at the Edge. If they hear something, they ring a small bell.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient. Tired. Aware that their job is the most consequential in the realm and that they will probably never be needed. They are fine with this.

characters.name: Isa
characters.portrait_blurb: Silver-white hair to her back. Frost-blue eyes. Barefoot on ice. Speaks like she is recovering from a long held breath.
characters.tribe_look: Cipher-souls. Each one is a letter of the Author's true name. Marked subtly — a flicker at the edge of vision when their letter is spoken.
characters.tribe_customs: Do not collide. Do not interfere with each other's realms. Walk only when the cipher calls.
characters.tribe_persona: Each unhurried in their own way. Each holds a corner of the universe the others cannot.

characters.name: Opha
characters.portrait_blurb: Heterochromia (violet + rose-pink). Half-cloak (Codexia ink-black, Astralis rose-pink with star-fragments). Cannot lie.
characters.tribe_look: Cipher-souls. Each one is a letter of the Author's true name. Marked subtly — a flicker at the edge of vision when their letter is spoken.
characters.tribe_customs: Do not collide. Do not interfere with each other's realms. Walk only when the cipher calls.
characters.tribe_persona: Each unhurried in their own way. Each holds a corner of the universe the others cannot.

characters.name: Runa
characters.portrait_blurb: Ancient. White braid to ankles. Sap-color eyes. Palms covered in burned-in rune-scars. Lives inside Ferdinand's bark.
characters.tribe_look: Nine guardians of Ferdinand's bark. Each carries one letter of the family name. Each meets the others once per cosmic year, on the night the moon stands beneath Ferdinand's lowest branch.
characters.tribe_customs: Vote unanimously each year on who speaks for the tree. Never raise their voices. Never break a promise to Ferdinand.
characters.tribe_persona: Patient. Watching. Older than they look. Each holds a piece of the Empire that no Architect can replace.

characters.name: Ögar
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall, gaunt. Blindfold woven from cypress thread and Mingus-hair. Beneath it: silver-coin eyes that see 8,000 timelines per heartbeat.
characters.tribe_look: Nine guardians of Ferdinand's bark. Each carries one letter of the family name. Each meets the others once per cosmic year, on the night the moon stands beneath Ferdinand's lowest branch.

characters.tribe_persona: Patient. Watching. Older than they look. Each holds a piece of the Empire that no Architect can replace.

characters.name: Niva
characters.portrait_blurb: Short, weathered, dark Sami features. Bone-white parka stitched with reindeer sinew. Silver-coin eyes. Single wooden stake.

characters.name: Njord
characters.portrait_blurb: Weathered Nordic fisherman. Salt-bleached blonde-grey beard to his chest. Sea-blue cloak. Wooden tide-rod with seven knots.

characters.name: Kvar
characters.portrait_blurb: Middle-aged. Ordinary face. Tired eyes that have seen too many endings. Patched leather coat with too many burn-marks. No weapon.

characters.name: Vidar
characters.portrait_blurb: Massive. Robed and hooded. Hands the size of plowshares. No face has been seen. The hood does not move when he breathes.

characters.name: Iska
characters.portrait_blurb: Child-sized but ancient. Copper-red hair in twin braids. Heavily freckled. Carries a small clay lamp glowing white flame.

characters.name: Tova
characters.portrait_blurb: Tall, athletic. Copper armor with engraved bell-plates. Raven-black single braid. Brass herald's horn slung across her chest. Dawn-gold eyes.
